questions: I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My colleague asks me to cover their shift this weekend because their kid has a school thing.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My neighbor asks if I can take in their mail while they're out of town for five days.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. A friend I haven't seen in a while texts to ask if I want to grab coffee sometime this week.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My sister asks what I want for my birthday this year.

I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. A coworker I work with daily sends me a message saying they enjoyed working on the last project together.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My brother calls to say he's going to be in town next week and asks if we can meet up for dinner.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. A friend calls to let me know they passed their exam after months of studying.

I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My roommate says they're going to be moving out in a few weeks and wanted to give me plenty of notice.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. An old friend sends me a birthday message saying they think about me often.
I keep people at arm's length — I don't get sentimental and I don't need closeness to function well. My mom calls just to check in and asks how I've been doing lately.
